notes: Aluminum 7075-T6 per MIL-A-12576. Higher strength than 7039 (ultimate ~570 MPa) but over-aging beyond ~75mm reduces ballistic performance. RHAe ~0.45-0.50 per mm vs small arms per MIL-HDBK-5. Used in limited armor applications where higher strength-to-weight ratio than 5083 is needed. Susceptible to stress corrosion cracking (SCC).

source.methodology: RHAe from MIL-HDBK-5J ballistic limit curves for 7075-T6, normalized to RHA thickness. Strength and density per MIL-A-12576.
